apply: Create and interpret different types of charts and graphs (bar charts, pie charts, line charts, histograms) to present data effectively.
Analyze and interpret data presented in different forms (tables, charts, graphs) to draw inferences and make conclusions.
Use statistical tools and techniques to identify trends and patterns in data.
analyze: Analyze the strengths and limitations of different data presentation methods and choose the most appropriate method for a given situation.
Evaluate the effectiveness of different data visualization techniques in conveying complex information.
Identify and analyze biases and errors in data presentation and interpretation.
remember: Identify and explain different types of data (primary, secondary, qualitative, and quantitative) and their sources.
List and describe various methods of data collection (surveys, questionnaires, observations, experiments) and their advantages/disadvantages.
Explain the concept of statistics and its importance in decision-making.
understand: Describe the process of data presentation, including data collection, compilation, and analysis.
Explain the concept of data visualization and its importance in understanding complex data.
Identify and describe different types of charts and graphs (bar charts, pie charts, line charts, histograms) and their uses.

ncert_objectives: To understand the different types of data and their sources.
To learn about the methods of data collection and their advantages/disadvantages.
To understand the concept of statistics and its importance in decision-making.
board_exam_objectives: To identify and describe different types of data and their sources.
To create and interpret different types of charts and graphs.
To analyze and interpret data presented in different forms and draw inferences.
